title: Duck boat overturns in Cambridge, injuring 11 people
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A Boston duck boat overturned in Cambridge, injuring 11 people, after a mechanical issue led to towing complications.
The duck boat named Molly Mollasses flipped on a boat ramp near 10 Museum Way, causing one serious injury.
Boston Duck Tours suspended operations and are working with authorities to investigate the incident.

title: Why JJ Peterka was worth the price of 2 1st-round picks for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins trade two first-round picks for winger JJ Peterka, addressing scoring and speed needs.
Peterka, 24, averaged 26 goals per season over the last three years with Buffalo and Utah.
Peterka's speed and offensive skills fit the Bruins' goal to add depth with faster, skilled players.

title: MassHealth will stop covering weight-loss drugs next week
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: MassHealth will stop paying for weight-loss drugs like Wegovy and Zepbound starting July 1, impacting 22,000 members.
The policy change aims to save $15 million annually amid rising healthcare costs, as stated in Gov. Maura Healey’s 2027 budget proposal.
Massachusetts previously covered these drugs under Medicaid without another medical condition; paying out of pocket remains challenging for many beneficiaries.

title: Here’s the 7 prospects Bruins added on Day 2 of 2026 NHL Draft
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins drafted seven players in Rounds 2-7 of the 2026 NHL Draft, focusing on goalies and skilled wingers.
Boston's notable picks include goalie Yuri Ivanov (No. 56), winger Nils Bartholdsson (No. 88), and defenseman Jacob Vandeven (No. 157).
Roberto Henriquez, a Boston College commit, was selected in the sixth round, enhancing the Bruins' goaltending prospects.

title: Native Venezuelan Willson Contreras speaks on devastating earthquakes: ‘I always keep Venezuela in my heart’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude earthquakes in Venezuela result in 1,430 deaths and 68,900 missing, says Associated Press.
Red Sox players, including Willson Contreras, wear hats displaying messages supporting earthquake-impacted Venezuela during games.
David Ortiz raises awareness and funds for Venezuela relief efforts at Fenway Park through the Red Sox Foundation.

title: Jaylen Brown responds to report that he’d be the 7th-best player on an NBA team according to analytics
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown criticized analytics that ranked him as the seventh-best player on an NBA team via a social media post.
NBA insider Bobby Marks shared team skepticism about Brown's analytics but later clarified his own respect for Brown's abilities.
Amid trade rumors, Brown is valued as a top player, with overwhelming support against negative analytic assessments of his performance.

title: Red Sox jump on Gerrit Cole early to defeat Yankees in third straight game
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox secure a series win by defeating Yankees 4-1, marking their third consecutive victory over New York.
Gerrit Cole allows four runs over 5.1 innings, continuing his struggles against Boston with a career ERA above six.
Jake Bennett shines for Boston, allowing only three hits and earning his second win in his sixth career start.

title: Bruins trade Fabian Lysell to Colorado for forward Ivan Ivan
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins traded 2021 first-round pick Fabian Lysell to Colorado Avalanche for forward Ivan Ivan.
Ivan Ivan played 66 AHL games in 2025-26, scoring 11 goals and 15 assists, and appeared in 49 games for the Avalanche.
Fabian Lysell scored one goal and two assists in 12 NHL games with Boston, achieving 163 points in 219 AHL games.

title: Forget the celebrities in the crowd, France are the entertainers of the 2026 World Cup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France defeated Norway 4-1 in a World Cup match at Gillette Stadium, showcasing their impressive attacking style.
French player Ousmane Dembélé scored a hat-trick, contributing to France's position as World Cup entertainers.
Despite a higher xG, Norway lost due to France's exceptional offensive performance led by Kylian Mbappe and others.

title: Cape DA investigating alleged threats at Sandwich Middle High
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sandwich Police and Cape & Islands DA are investigating threats made by a student at Sandwich Middle High School.
A school safety app flagged threatening language, alerting administrators and prompting a police investigation into the incident.
The student involved has not returned to school, and authorities informed families of the targeted classmates.
